entities.name: FERONIA
entities.description: A cell wall receptor-like kinase (RLK) that acts as a sensor and bridge between apoplast and cytosolic signaling pathways in root biology

entities.name: ROOT GROWTH
entities.description: The process of root development and expansion in plants

entities.name: NUTRIENT UPTAKE
entities.description: The process by which roots absorb nutrients and water from the soil

entities.name: STRESS SENSING
entities.description: The ability of roots to perceive and respond to abiotic stressors and mechanical stimuli

entities.name: MICROBIOME ASSEMBLY
entities.description: The process of shaping and organizing the microbial community around the roots

entities.name: ROOT SYSTEM ARCHITECTURE (RSA)
entities.description: The spatial configuration of the root system in the soil

entities.name: HIGH-THROUGHPUT PHENOTYPING
entities.description: Tools used for the rapid and automated measurement of plant traits

entities.name: GENOTYPING TOOLS
entities.description: Tools used to determine the genetic makeup of an organism

entities.name: APOPLAST
entities.description: The space outside the plasma membrane where cell wall components are located

entities.name: CYTOSOLIC SIGNALING PATHWAYS
entities.description: Intracellular signal transduction pathways occurring in the cytosol

entities.name: RECEPTOR-LIKE KINASE (RLK)
entities.description: A class of proteins that act as sensors to trigger intracellular signaling

entities.name: RAPID ALKALINIZATION FACTOR (RALF) PEPTIDES
entities.description: Peptides involved in the regulation of root traits via FERONIA

entities.name: CALCIUM
entities.description: An ion involved in signaling pathways controlled by FERONIA

entities.name: REACTIVE OXYGEN SPECIES (ROS)
entities.description: Chemically reactive molecules involved in signaling pathways controlled by FERONIA

entities.name: HORMONAL SIGNALING PATHWAYS
entities.description: Pathways involving plant hormones that regulate root responses

entities.name: ABIOTIC STRESSORS
entities.description: Non-living environmental factors that cause stress to the plant

entities.name: MECHANICAL STIMULI
entities.description: Physical forces that trigger responses in the root system

entities.name: CLIMATE RESILIENT CROPS
entities.description: Crops developed to maintain high yields under adverse environmental conditions
